1.q: SbMTA-overexpressed sorghum displayed increased activities of ROS scavenging enzymes such as CAT, SOD, and POD... SbMTA overexpression enhances the capacity to scavenge ROS under salt stress.

3.q: overexpression of SbVPPase in Bacopa alleviated salt stress by sequestering Na+.

4.q: SbCASP4-expression lines had... higher expression levels of the genes related to Casparian strip formation... SbCASP4 can enhance salt tolerance by regulating apoplastic barrier formation

5.q: physiological photosynthesis parameters, chlorophyll content, PSII photochemical efficiency, and PSI oxidoreductive activity in the wild type decreased more severely than in the overexpression lines but less than in T-DNA insertion mutants under salt stress.

8.q: Yeast two-hybrid screening result showed that SbMYBAS1 might interact with proteins encoded by SORBI_302G184600, SORBI_3009G247900 and SORBI_3004G59600.

9.q: Yeast two-hybrid screening result showed that SbMYBAS1 might interact with proteins encoded by SORBI_302G184600, SORBI_3009G247900 and SORBI_3004G59600.

10.q: Yeast two-hybrid screening result showed that SbMYBAS1 might interact with proteins encoded by SORBI_302G184600, SORBI_3009G247900 and SORBI_3004G59600.

11.q: Both yeast two-hybrid and BiFC experiments confirmed the interaction between SbbHLH85 and PHF1.

12.q: SbCRY1a interacted directly with a putative RING E3 ubiquitin ligase constitutive photomorphogenesis 1 (COP1) from sweet sorghum (SbCOP1) instead of SbSPA1 to suppress SbCOP1–SbHY5 interaction responding to blue light.

13.q: these four genes were named SbHKT1;3, SbHKT1;4, SbHKT1;5, and SbHKT2;1, respectively ... members of the HKT gene family from Sorghum bicolor.

18.q: The overexpression of SbbHLH85 led to a decrease in the expression of PHF1 as well as the content of Pi.

19.q: SbWRKY55 binds directly to the promoter of SbBGLU22 and inhibits its expression level.

20.q: When proSbSOS1-pLacZi2u was co-transformed with GAD-SbWRKY50, LacZ expression was activated; similarly strong fluorescence signals were observed, indicating SbWRKY50 directly binds SbSOS1 promoter.

21.q: When proSbHKT1-pLacZi2u was co-transformed with GAD-SbWRKY50, LacZ expression was activated and strong fluorescence signals were observed, indicating SbWRKY50 directly binds SbHKT1 promoter.

22.q: Yeast one-hybrid assays and dual-luciferase reporter assay showed that SbWRKY55 binds directly to the promoter of SbBGLU22 and inhibits its expression level.

23.q: These results strongly suggest that plasma membrane bound SbNHXLP involves in Na+ exclusion, maintains ion homeostasis in transgenics in comparison with WT and alleviates NaCl stress.

24.q: SbCASP-LP1C1 may be involved in the polymerization of lignin monomers in the Casparian strip of sweet sorghum, thereby regulating salt tolerance.

25.q: SbCRY1a-overexpressed transgenic Arabidopsis showed oversensitive to abscisic acid (ABA) and salinity. The ABA-responsive gene ABI5 was up-regulated evidently in SbCRY1a transgenic lines.

26.q: Both in vivo and in vitro biochemical analyses showed that SbWRKY55 interacts with the FYVE zinc finger protein SbFYVE1, blocking the ABA signaling pathway.

27.q: blue light-enhanced SbCRY1a–SbCOP1 interaction suppressed SbCOP1–SbHY5 interaction

28.q: both in vivo and in vitro biochemical analyses showed that SbWRKY55 interacts with the FYVE zinc finger protein SbFYVE1, blocking the ABA signaling pathway.

29.q: NADP-ME is a critical enzyme of the C4 pathway. The main role of this enzyme is to catalyze oxidative decarboxylation of malic acid to provide CO2 for the photosynthetic carbon fixation of the Rubisco enzyme.

30.q: SbWRKY55 functions as a key component of the ABA-mediated signaling pathway; SbWRKY55 plays a dual role in regulating ABA-mediated responses to salt stress in sorghum.

31.q: a really interesting new gene (RING)-H2 type E3 ligase, which regulates target proteins via the Ub/26S proteasome system, was isolated from a rice plant

32.q: plasma membrane bound SbNHXLP involves in Na+ exclusion, maintains ion homeostasis in transgenics in comparison with WT and alleviates NaCl stress.

33.q: These results indicate that SbCASP-LP1C1 may be involved in the polymerization of lignin monomers in the Casparian strip of sweet sorghum, thereby regulating salt tolerance.

34.q: miR-6225-5p reduced the level of Ca2+ in the miR-6225-5p-overexpressing line by inhibiting the expression of the Ca2+ uptake gene SbGLR3.1 in the root epidermis and affected salt tolerance in sorghum.
